title: Chapter 106 – Lightning Genius' Legacy (6)
content:
"What... did he cultivate the Wudang Sect's cultivation technique?"

Ma Se-in muttered in disbelief. The assembled elders stirred visibly.

Several faces changed color. They didn't resemble people who had carried such dense energy pathways.

They looked as though they wanted to speak immediately.

"Closer to Shaolin than Wudang. But even Shaolin wouldn't compress and gather energy like that."

Lazy Flame Dragon murmured to himself.

"So you're Lazy Flame Dragon. 'Wouldn't?' That's the wrong word. 'Can't' is more accurate. There is talent even in the gathering of energy."

Only Ma Yeon-jeok nodded, a smile playing across his face.

Meanwhile, a cold voice cut through the room.

"Did you just speak of rights and demands?"

Zhu Lianting's tone was severe. Unlike her son Ma Se-in, her eyes wavered only once before fixing on Jeong Yeon-shin with a gaze that was straight and unyielding.

She showed no weakness, even with a purple-rank supreme master standing beside her.

Though she surely knew of Ma Yeon-jeok's affection for Jeong Yeon-shin.

Sure enough, Ma Yeon-jeok's thunderous rebuke descended.

"Who do you think you're interrogating? Step back."

"Lightning Genius, that child invoked the family name while speaking of rights and demands. This is Ma family business. Do you intend to hand over your liver and gall to some Radiant Demon Wing blue-rank, bound by private sentiment?"

"What did you just say?"

"Family Head, please temper your anger and show understanding."

The argument between Ma Yeon-jeok and Zhu Lianting erupted. Zhu Lianting didn't so much as twitch an eyebrow.

Only Ma Yeon-jeok's face flushed with rage.

Zhu Lianting's position was stronger than expected.

That much was clear from the elders' grim silence -- none spoke for or against either side.

Though they say the inner politics of a great noble family are no different from those of the Forbidden City, it was hard to fathom given Ma Yeon-jeok's stature.

Even if his martial prowess had declined, he was still the former Divine Sword Group Leader.

--Did your mother pass on imperial family martial arts? This scene doesn't make sense.

Jeong Yeon-shin openly sent a sound transmission. The question was directed at his branch cousin. The reply came at once.

--Your insight is rather sharp.

Ma Se-in spoke with a bitter face. Complicated feelings lay buried in his answer.

The Ma family's martial arts were not orthodox methods.

Recently, Ma Yeon-jeok's declining skill had become increasingly obvious, and he was the family's only purple-rank master.

The Desolate Ma family had begun reconsidering its hereditary martial arts.

The rise and fall of prestigious murim families was always directly tied to the level and nature of their martial arts.

Meanwhile, Zhu Lianting had introduced the imperial Golden Immortal Eight Methods.

The moment Ma Se-in was chosen as heir, she had astoundingly obtained the imperial family's permission to partially release the Golden Immortal Eight Methods verses.

With that, she won the favor of the Ma family retainers and built a formidable base of support.

Ma Se-in's position could no longer be shaken by Ma Yeon-jeok's personal whims.

--I see.

Jeong Yeon-shin nodded inwardly.

It had struck him as strange when Zhu Lianting had circumvented Ma Yeon-jeok to contact him directly, but now everything fell into place.

The Desolate Ma family was a martial sect.

Prestigious family authority sprang from martial arts and renown.

When someone held the martial arts that formed the family's very foundation, everyone else had no choice but to yield.

'Not my concern right now.'

At this very moment, someone from this family faced a crisis like a candle in the wind in Sichuan's murim.

Jeong Yeon-shin had obtained the raw intelligence fastest through the Administration. He spoke without hesitation.

"Radiant Demon Wing Leader Ma Jin."

His voice cut through Ma Yeon-jeok and Zhu Lianting's argument.

Earlier, he had vaporized the Treasure Spirit Jade before everyone's eyes.

Jeong Yeon-shin's voice carried undeniable presence.

"They say Uncle Ma's whereabouts are unknown. Radiant Demon Wing as well. The Administration said they must be considered a force out of commission."

"What..."

One of the Desolate Ma family elders spoke first. It seemed an involuntary response. Jeong Yeon-shin continued without pause.

"Urgent news just reached the Administration. I heard it directly. We need forces capable of finding and recovering the entire Radiant Demon Wing."

"..."

After a moment of silence, someone reacted. The energy that rose was fierce.

The purple robes surging like raging waves belonged to Ma Yeon-jeok.

"...Do you understand how chaotic the murim has become? It's because the famine has dragged on too long. Countless people are rising up across the land -- it's become nearly impossible to tell martial artists from commoners. Trash who learned a handful of techniques now act as petty kings across the central plains. The main fortress has no capacity to spare. Radiant Demon Wing must demonstrate their survival through their own strength."

His momentum and his words stood at odds. Even as he radiated anger, he maintained razor-sharp reason.

He could separate public from private, even when facing the grandson he owed so much to.

The former Divine Sword Group Leader's temperament ran cold. Values hardened through decades of duty.

For a moment, Jeong Yeon-shin couldn't see his grandfather as human.

Had he been like this when he abandoned Mother? The old sword of Desolate Fortress moved his wrinkled lips toward the boy.

"The Administration must have said the same. Am I right?"

"...Yes. That's why I thought to borrow the Ma family's hand."

Jeong Yeon-shin answered.

"...The main family's forces are also deployed for civilian relief across the land. By the Lord's command. With the Eight Families and Thirteen Evil Sects each running wild, the main fortress barely has a few dozen fighters left..."

"Can't Grandfather go out directly?"

He finally spoke the words that had been circling in his mouth.

The one who answered was Zhu Lianting. Her red lips twisted into a strange shape.

"Purple masters without a formal position must remain on standby. That is the main fortress's law."

Everyone spoke of law. All were bound by it.

Suddenly, Jeong Yeon-shin felt an impulse surge through him. He wanted to shatter something he couldn't see.

'What should I do?'

He needed forces capable of handling Sichuan's murim. And he didn't have much time.

Radiant Demon Wing's whereabouts were already unknown.

Every moment of delay widened the possibility that they wouldn't even find bodies.

'Should I accept the Desolate Ma family martial arts and revise them? Even if it takes time.'

No. Impossible.

How long would formulas accumulated over generations require? It wasn't something that could be finished quickly.

Dismantling and rebuilding the Ma family orthodoxy from scratch would actually take less time.

But who here would agree to that?

"Ah..."

His chest felt strangled. It was an unsolvable impasse.

Jeong Yeon-shin tasted helplessness for the first time since learning about his shortened lifespan.

Perhaps it was all the more bitter because it wasn't something he could confront directly in Sichuan.

"This woman has privately raised forces. Promise me one thing, and I'll gladly lend them to you."

Zhu Lianting's cold voice reached his ears alone. She was a royal branch member.

Like a serpent -- impossible to predict what demands she might make.

It was a proposal to return to only if he failed to secure forces before the night was over.

"Hang in there."

He stepped away with Lazy Flame Dragon offering awkward comfort. The Desolate Ma family watched him depart in silence.

Most wore hardened expressions, as though they too were struggling to master their hearts.

***

It was something that couldn't be helped. Everyone seemed to say as much.

Step.

Jeong Yeon-shin hadn't given up.

'By whatever means necessary.'

He already had certain people in mind. Himself and Lazy Flame Dragon first. He planned to plead with Namgung Hwa-shin as well.

'Start with three blue masters secured.'

But the search and inquiry had to begin the moment they entered Sichuan. Manpower was lacking.

Even white-rank warriors could overwhelm ordinary wanderers, but there was no way to muster forces on the spot.

He was tallying numbers alone when the boy suddenly raised his head.

He sensed presences gathered in the distance. Beyond the Desolate Ma family's main gate, he felt waves of qi settled in calm formation.

Not just one or two. From warriors without formal Desolate Fortress rank to those who could extend their swords in the blue realm.

"They say they're guests who came looking for you, Yeon-shin."

Ma Yeon-jeok seemed to have sensed them first. His tone was peculiar.

Guwoong!

They seemed to have detected Jeong Yeon-shin's presence from outside as well. The massive gate slowly swung open.

A group approached. Straight toward Jeong Yeon-shin.

"Their affiliations are all different...?"

Ma Se-in's voice rang with bewilderment. As the Desolate Ma family heir, he had spent long years at the main fortress and recognized the faces of the Divine Sword Squads' fighting forces.

'The ones in front are all formal warriors.'

Jeong Yeon-shin thought. About thirty? Many unnamed disciples were visible behind them as well.

The boy's gaze swept over them. There was a familiar physicality in their approach. All of it was familiar.

What he had only felt within his own body was now reflected in theirs. Traces of ceaselessly accumulated training.

Muscle structure, gait, the faint energy of practiced techniques.

"Lightning Genius? He's even... younger than I'd heard."

"If you're judging by appearance, look at that physique first."

"An amazingly well-trained body. Worth the effort of coming here."

"Worth it? Shouldn't finding the underlying principle come first? He gave us something impossible -- we should repay that first."

They were the ones approaching from the right. They seemed to have specialized in fist techniques.

The muscle contours below their shoulders traced elegant curves. The lines were familiar.

Those were body changes prominent in early Eternal Blossom Fist training. Cultivating with qi produced exactly that effect.

'So Shin So-bin's words were true.'

He could even identify which techniques they had focused on.

Four had properly cultivated the First Move -- Spiral. Three had focused on the Second Move -- Advancing Thunder. The remaining five trained in the Third Move -- Blossoming Fist.

Desolate Fortress's warriors were all exceptional talents. He had heard as much from the Radiant Demon Wing seniors.

Each of them had been called a genius in their hometown.

Given one technique to cultivate over a short period, they could achieve clear results.

'And.'

Jeong Yeon-shin's gaze shifted.

"..."

Those walking opposite the fist technique warriors were silent.

They were swordsmen. The Desolate Swords at their waists said everything.

Both men and women had long limbs. Familiar qualities emanated from their entire bearing.

Sharp energy like rays of sunlight. They seemed to have trained several moves of the Radiant Sword Style.

'That's not all.'

Some even carried the distinct vitality unique to the Fate Defying Scripture.

Had they truly entered arts requiring such delicate sensitivity to qi layering?

Others still showed the principles of Radiant Wing Step in their very footwork.

His vision was filled with his own martial arts. Living, breathing martial verses given form.

Jeong Yeon-shin stood motionless for a moment.

"Why do you look like that? You were truly like a blade at the promotion test."

A heavy voice rang out. An energy like that of a veteran northern army general wafted powerfully from the speaker.

It was the man standing at the front of the approaching group.

The middle-aged warrior's scarred face was familiar.

His clothes were not. At their first meeting he had worn white, but now he wore blue.

Crimson Day Sword of Desolate Fortress's Annihilation Team.

He had crossed swords with Jeong Yeon-shin during the blue-rank promotion examination.

Crimson Day Sword, too, seemed to have finally advanced to blue.

"After your return, we tracked your movements. And today, we finally heard you'd left the Radiant Demon Wing hall."

Crimson Day Sword stopped about ten paces from Jeong Yeon-shin.

Despite the terrible scars carved into his face, his expression was gentle.

"We searched for quite a while."

His speech had changed. Not the tone one used when addressing Lightning Genius the white-rank warrior. He was no longer looking down.

The warriors who had approached with Crimson Day Sword all stopped as well.

Those who pursued martial arts in Desolate Fortress took in the sight of one boy.

'Strange, yet familiar.'

Jeong Yeon-shin thought. That was the feeling.

Not strangers. That was what martial artists were. Arts give rise to sects; martial arts connect people.

And from that, something is born. What they call martial arts sects.

Strong martial. Vein of legacy.

Even at this very moment, a martial arts sect was being created.

Martial arts donation. What had been given freely in pursuit of merit now returned with astonishing results.

"Let's pay our respects."

Crimson Day Sword said.

In an instant, every warrior raised clasped hands to their chests. It was a disciplined greeting.

A bearing that anyone could tell came from the heart. It carried a respect beyond embellishment.

The feeling that washed over him was strange. The Ma family figures -- Zhu Lianting included -- receded from Jeong Yeon-shin's mind.

Even though all of them were watching this scene unfold.

"Practitioners of one legacy."

As if rehearsed. The moment Crimson Day Sword began, the warriors lined up behind him opened their mouths in unison.

--We humbly pay our respects to the Grandmaster.

Speaking with one voice.

It was a greeting that awakened the night of Desolate Fortress.
